title: genista ferox var. ifniensis
intro: Genista ferox var. ifniensis, also known as Ifni broom, is a rare and lesser-known species of broom shrub native to the Canary Islands and western Morocco. It belongs to the Fabaceae family and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin irritations, thanks to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be found in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to promote wound healing and support overall well-being.
